note: The panels below are illustrative — two small groups rating the same three openings, one on gut feeling and one using the rubric. The agreement numbers on the page are COMPUTED from these ratings in your browser (share of rater-pairs landing within one point). Swap in your class's real ratings and every number updates.

rubric.name: Fit
rubric.definition: Does it suit its job — an opening that makes you want the next line?

rubric.name: Form
rubric.definition: Do the parts work together — rhythm, image, and sense pulling one way?

rubric.name: Earned surprise
rubric.definition: Is the unexpected part doing real work, or just being weird?
rubric.s5: a surprise that opens the whole story

rubric.name: Return
rubric.definition: Would you willingly read it a second time?

items.text: It was a dark and stormy night, and Sarah couldn't sleep.
The last time I saw my father, he was teaching a pigeon to trust him.
Monday arrived like a returned library book: overdue, faintly damp, and nobody's favorite.
